# LaneKeep

> Governance sidecar for AI coding agents

LaneKeep evaluates every tool call through a multi-tier pipeline before it
executes. Rules, limits, audit trail. Your agent runs within boundaries that
you define.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): Quick start, configuration, CLI reference
- [REFERENCE](REFERENCE.md): Full config reference — rules, policies, budget, env vars
- [Developer Guide](CLAUDE.md): Architecture, evaluators, hooks, testing
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): Setup, workflow, PR checklist
- [Security](SECURITY.md): Vulnerability reporting, defense layers
- [Plugin Authoring](plugins.d/AUTHORING.md): Write custom evaluators (Bash + polyglot)

## Core Concepts

- 9-tier evaluation pipeline (hardblock, rules, hidden text, PII, budget, plugins, semantic)
- 140 default rules (+ 58 Windows rules on Windows platforms) across 13 categories
- First-match-wins rule engine with AND-logic match fields
- Three enforcement profiles: strict, guided (default), autonomous
- JSON everywhere: config, wire protocol, audit trail
- Zero network calls in core evaluation path
- Plugin system: Bash and polyglot (Python, JS) with subshell isolation
- Append-only JSONL audit trail — every decision logged
- Claude Code support via hook adapters; Cursor adapter included (untested)
